title: Understanding the Pythagorean Theorem
text: The Pythagorean Theorem is a fundamental principle in geometry that relates the lengths of the sides of a right triangle. It states that in a right triangle, the square of the length of the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle) is equal to the sum of the squares of the lengths of the other two sides.

title: Identifying the Right Triangle
text: To apply the Pythagorean Theorem, first ensure that you are working with a right triangle. A right triangle has one angle that is exactly $$90$$ degrees.

title: Labeling the Sides
text: In a right triangle, label the sides as follows: 'a' and 'b' are the legs (the two shorter sides), and 'c' is the hypotenuse (the longest side).

title: Applying the Pythagorean Theorem
text: Use the formula $$a^2+b^2=c^2$$ to find the missing side of the triangle. If you know the lengths of the two legs, you can find the hypotenuse. If you know one leg and the hypotenuse, you can find the other leg.

title: Solving for the Missing Side
text: Rearrange the Pythagorean Theorem formula to solve for the missing side. For example, if you need to find 'a', rearrange the formula to $$a=\sqrt{c^2-b^2}$$.

title: Checking Your Work
text: After calculating the missing side, substitute the values back into the Pythagorean Theorem to ensure that the equation holds true. This will confirm that your solution is correct.
